name: rasa.domain.core
description:
RasaOS Domain · Core. The stripped-down canonical template every domain Element follows. Fork this when authoring a new domain (rasa.domain.legal, rasa.domain.code, rasa.domain.health, etc.) and populate content/skills/, content/rules/, content/agents/ + seed/ with the domain-specific material. Holds zero domain knowledge — it's the SHAPE every domain shares.

Distinct from rasa.core (kind: core, the L1 shared bones every Element depends on). domain.core is the template for the domain KIND; rasa.core is the L1 dependency. Different concerns, both named 'core' for canonical positioning.

rasa.note: Stripped-down canonical template for the domain kind. Forked-from-this becomes the starting point for any new domain Element. As of v0.1.0 truly stripped — content/ subdirectories are present but empty (.gitkeep only); seed/ ships only the canonical rasa.lock.json.template + CLAUDE.md.template; bin/init does the minimum (parse rasa.json, apply install policies for whatever content/seed entries the fork declares, stamp rasa.lock.json). Phase 2 of domain-core's design will lock down the UNIFIED shape across domain-code + domain-legal (the two existing domain implementations) and version those decisions in this Element.

element.description: Files mirrored into the install target. Per canon ELEMENT_CONTRACT.md §7 install policies.

seed.description: One-time files copied at install only. Per canon ELEMENT_CONTRACT.md §7.

files.note: Per-project Claude session contract. Lives in the install target's root; project-owned forever.
Stamped with the Element HEAD commit SHA at init time. Lockfile shape per canon Spec §6 (Connection Contract). Substitutes {{ELEMENT_SHA}}, {{ELEMENT_BRANCH}}, {{TODAY}} placeholders.

scaffold.description: Empty directories created at init. Forks populate as needed.
